summary: Subject is Fred Rocba, a remote technical engineer at Stark Research Labs (SRL)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx speaker notes: Fred Rocba, a technical engineer, was hired at Stark Research Labs (SRL) in October 2020 and worked from home. Windows 10, single user 'fredr'.

summary: SRL Office365 mailbox frocba@stark-research-labs.com (the corporate IP-access identity)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx: Office365 mailbox frocba@stark-research-labs.com; SRL is a biotech/metals/advanced-alloy R&D lab and a repeated nation-state target.

summary: Remote access via RDP — the work-from-home and intrusion vector (Q4: how)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx: Fred Rocba worked from home via RDP + SaaS; the home was broken into and the SRL laptop (left logged in) was targeted. Remote-access/RDP is the access method.

summary: Cloud-sync exfiltration channel: Dropbox (Q3: where transferred to)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx: personal Dropbox populated; SaaS used from home. Dropbox is a candidate transfer destination for stolen IP.

summary: Cloud-sync exfiltration channel: OneDrive (Q3: where transferred to)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx: OneDrive populated. A candidate transfer destination for stolen IP.

summary: Office365 / cloud email as an IP movement channel (Q3: where transferred to)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx: worked via Office365; personal accounts fred.rocba@gmail.com and fred.rocba@outlook.com. Email/cloud is a transfer channel.

summary: Intrusion window: subject left on vacation 2020-11-10; break-in 2020-11-13 (Q5: when)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx: leaves on vacation 2020-11-10; home broken into the evening of 2020-11-13. Suspicious activity should cluster in/after this window while the user was absent.

summary: Anti-forensic / secure-deletion tooling (SDelete) consistent with covering the theft
source_excerpt: Break-in + IP theft scenario: secure-deletion / anti-forensic activity is an expected method/cover-up artifact for how the theft was executed and concealed.

summary: Privileged/backup account targeting during the intrusion (failed logons to BACKUPADMIN)
source_excerpt: IP-theft-by-intrusion: credential attacks against privileged/backup accounts (Security EventID 4625 failed logons) are an expected access method during the break-in window.

summary: SRL R&D intellectual property (advanced-alloy / metals research) is the stolen target (Q2)
source_excerpt: ROCBA-BACKGROUND.pptx: SRL is an advanced-alloy/metals biotech R&D lab; the case is IP theft. The stolen material is SRL research IP under the user's document tree.
